title: Employment Contract
content: An employment contract exists when a worker performs services under the authority and supervision of an employer in return for remuneration. The contract may be written or oral, explicit or implicit.
importance: Legally defines who must be declared to CNSS.

title: Fixed-Term Contract (CDD)
content: A fixed-term contract is allowed only in specific cases: temporary increase in activity, replacement of an absent employee, or seasonal work. Total duration must not exceed 4 years, including renewals.

title: Transformation into Open-Ended Contract (CDI)
content: If a fixed-term contract exceeds the legal duration or is renewed illegally, it is automatically considered an open-ended contract (CDI).

title: Equal Rights for CDD Employees
content: Employees hired under a CDD must receive the same salary as permanent employees and benefit from the same working conditions.

title: Trial Period
content: A trial period may be included in the employment contract. During this period, either party may terminate the contract without compensation but must respect the legal notice period.

title: Temporary Suspension
content: The employment contract is suspended (not terminated) in cases such as illness, accident, or maternity leave. After the suspension period, the employee has the right to return to their position.

title: Termination Rules
content: Termination must be justified by a real and serious cause and respect the legal notice period (préavis).

title: Abusive Dismissal
content: A dismissal is abusive if it has no legal or factual justification. In such cases, the employer may be required to pay damages.
